content:
JWT Authentication in nodejs

Learn jwt authentication nodejs with a complete nodejs example. Copy, adapt, and test with our free JWT tools.

Install Dependencies

npm install jsonwebtoken

Sign a JWT

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
// Sign
const token = jwt.sign({ sub: 'user-1' }, process.env.JWT_SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });
// Verify
try {
  const decoded = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET);
} catch (err) {
  console.error('jwt authentication nodejs:', err.message);
}

Verify a JWT

Always verify the signature and validate exp, iss, and aud claims. Test your tokens with JWT Validator.

Debug Issues

If verification fails, use JWT Debugger and see our invalid signature guide.

faq.a: jwt authentication nodejs is a common JWT authentication topic. This guide explains the concept with step-by-step instructions, code examples, and links to free decoder and validator tools.

faq.q: Are JWT tools on this site free?
faq.a: Yes. All 13 tools run client-side in your browser with no account required. Tokens are never uploaded to a server.

faq.q: How do I debug JWT errors?
faq.a: Use our JWT Decoder to inspect structure, JWT Validator to verify signatures, and JWT Debugger for claim-by-claim analysis and expiration warnings.

faq.q: Is decoding the same as validating a JWT?
faq.a: No. Decoding reads header and payload without proving authenticity. Always verify the signature before trusting claims in production.

faq.q: Which JWT algorithm should I use?
faq.a: Use RS256 or ES256 for public APIs and OAuth. HS256 is fine for internal services when you can protect the shared secret.
